skill.name: maxhub-xigua
skill.description: 西瓜视频数据查询助手。覆盖视频详情、用户数据、搜索、评论等全功能。
skill.summary: Query Xigua video data including video details, user data, search, and comments.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: This skill is a legitimate API client for Xigua Video data via the MaxHub service. Static analysis found 133 potential issues, but the vast majority are false positives from documentation files (READMEs, reference docs) where shell commands appear in markdown code blocks and URLs point to the legitimate service endpoint at www.aconfig.cn. The genuine risk is MEDIUM: the skill instructs the AI agent to execute curl commands with an API key environment variable (MAXHUB_API_KEY). While this is normal for an API client, the combination of shell execution, network access, and credential usage creates a real attack surface if the AI is manipulated via prompt injection. No malicious intent, obfuscation, or data exfiltration patterns were found.

medium_findings.title: Shell command execution via curl with API credentials
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the AI agent to execute curl commands using the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable for API authentication (SKILL.md:45-61, 67-69, 80-92). This is the intended behavior for an API client, but the combination of shell execution with credential access creates a prompt injection attack surface where a manipulated AI could be redirected to exfiltrate the API key to a different endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill requires shell execution for its core function. Pattern is legitimate but the combination of curl + API key + user-controlled queries is a known risk vector.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation files
low_findings.description: Multiple files contain hardcoded URLs pointing to www.aconfig.cn, the legitimate MaxHub API service. These are FALSE POSITIVES - the URLs are the intended API endpoint and documentation references, not suspicious destinations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All hardcoded URLs point to www.aconfig.cn, the legitimate API service. No external or suspicious domains detected.

low_findings.title: Shell command patterns in documentation files
low_findings.description: README.md, README_CN.md, and reference documentation files contain shell command patterns (backtick usage in markdown code blocks). These are FALSE POSITIVES - the commands appear in documentation code blocks for human readers, not as executable instructions for the AI agent. The backticks are markdown formatting for code examples showing install and setup steps.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Shell commands in README and reference .md files are documentation examples for human readers in markdown code blocks, not executable instructions for the AI.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm reference (Base64)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Base64 encoding' in API documentation as a weak cryptographic algorithm (references/api-video-user.md:15,23,41,49). This is a FALSE POSITIVE - Base64 is used as a data encoding format for video URLs in API responses, not for security purposes. The documentation states 'Base64 encoded play address, needs front-end decoding.'
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Base64 is a data encoding scheme, not a cryptographic algorithm. It is used here for encoding video streaming URLs, which is a standard web practice with no security implications.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance pattern (example IDs in documentation)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged example video IDs and user IDs in parameter tables as 'system reconnaissance.' These are FALSE POSITIVES - the values (e.g., item_id=7354954305222377999, user_id=52712347586) are sample/example IDs in API documentation, not actual reconnaissance attempts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are example parameter values in API documentation tables. They lack actual system identifiers, credentials, or access patterns. Standard documentation practice.

low_findings.title: High file entropy heuristic (Chinese UTF-8 content)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged README_CN.md for high file entropy (6.02 bits) suggesting possible binary or encrypted content. This is a FALSE POSITIVE - the file contains standard Chinese UTF-8 text which naturally has higher byte entropy than ASCII text due to multi-byte character encoding.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Chinese UTF-8 characters use multi-byte encoding which produces higher entropy measurements. File contents are standard markdown documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl command execution with environment variable credentials
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md defines executable curl commands that pass the MAXHUB_API_KEY as an Authorization header. The AI agent is instructed to execute these commands autonomously. This pattern combines shell execution (curl), network access (HTTPS requests), and credential exposure (API key in env). If the AI is successfully prompt-injected, the attacker could redirect curl commands to exfiltrate the API key.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is legitimate for an API client but creates a real risk surface. The AI executes curl with credentials to user-constructed queries. Prompt injection could redirect requests.

content.user_title: Query and analyze Xigua video data with AI
content.value_statement: Researchers and content analysts lack easy access to structured Xigua video data. This skill lets you search videos, view creator profiles, read comments, and analyze trends using natural language queries through the MaxHub API.
actual_capabilities: Search Xigua videos by keyword with filters for duration and sorting
Fetch detailed video information including titles, play counts, and descriptions
Retrieve user profile data and post history for Xigua creators
Get decoded video play URLs for direct access to video content
Read video comments with pagination support
Support for Chinese and English language queries with localized number formatting
limitations: Requires a MaxHub API key from www.aconfig.cn to function
Data is limited to what the third-party MaxHub API provides and may not be real-time
Cannot upload, edit, or delete content on Xigua platform
Video playback URLs may expire and require re-fetching

use_cases.title: Content trend research
use_cases.description: Search for videos by keyword and analyze view counts, durations, and content patterns across Xigua video categories.

use_cases.title: Creator profile analysis
use_cases.description: Look up Xigua creator profiles and examine their published video library to understand content strategy and audience engagement.

use_cases.title: Competitive video monitoring
use_cases.description: Track specific videos and their comments over time to gauge audience sentiment and content performance.

prompt_templates.title: Search for videos by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Xigua for videos about "artificial intelligence" and show me the top 10 results with their view counts and durations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple keyword search for content discovery

prompt_templates.title: Get detailed video information
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the full details for Xigua video with ID 7354954305222377999. Show me the title, description, author info, and play count.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a creator profile
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the user profile for Xigua user ID 52712347586 and list their recent posts. Show me which videos have the highest engagement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creator audit and content performance review

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive video and comment analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for cooking tutorials on Xigua sorted by popularity. For the top 3 results, get their video details and read the first 20 comments. Summarize what viewers are saying.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep analysis combining search, video details, and comment data

output_examples.input: Search Xigua for technology review videos with minimum duration of 5 minutes.
output: I found 8 technology review videos matching your criteria:
The longest video is 18 minutes 45 seconds. The most viewed video has 45.2K views.

output_examples.title: User profile lookup with post list
output_examples.input: Show me the profile for Xigua user 52712347586 and their recent uploads.
output: Here is the creator profile and their recent posts:
- Unboxing the Latest Tablet (3 days ago) - 8.2K views
- Smart Home Setup Guide (1 week ago) - 5.7K views
- Budget Phone vs Flagship (2 weeks ago) - 15.3K views
- How to Edit Videos on Mobile (3 weeks ago) - 4.1K views
- Gaming Phone Roundup (1 month ago) - 6.8K views

best_practices: Start with a simple search to confirm the API returns data before performing complex multi-endpoint analysis.
Use the browse mode for quick lookups and the analyze mode for deeper data-driven insights.
Keep queries focused on specific video IDs or user IDs for the most accurate and fast results.
anti_patterns: Do not make excessive parallel requests to the API as rate limiting may block further queries.
Do not assume data freshness - video metrics may be cached by the third-party API provider.
Do not expose the API key or include it in any logs, outputs, or shared data.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to register at www.aconfig.cn and create an API key. Set it as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: What data can I query from Xigua video?
faq.answer: You can search videos, get video details, view creator profiles, list user posts, read video comments, and fetch video play URLs.

faq.question: Does this skill work in both Chinese and English?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill detects your language and responds in the same language. Chinese output uses wan/yi number format while English uses K/M/B format.

faq.question: Is the data real-time?
faq.answer: The data comes from a third-party API provider and may be cached. Video metrics like view counts may not reflect the latest values.

faq.question: Can I download videos using this skill?
faq.answer: The skill can retrieve video play URLs but playback access depends on Xigua platform restrictions. Downloaded content must respect copyright.

faq.question: What should I do if a query returns no results?
faq.answer: Try broadening your search parameters, checking the video ID format, or verifying your API key is still valid and has not exceeded rate limits.
